_description: Corporate / Enterprise. Deep org hierarchy with SSO, delegation, service accounts, and teams. Orgs nest arbitrarily (Company → Division → Department → Team → Project). Each level can have its own IdP, hosted login, and permission scope. Best for: enterprise software, government, healthcare, financial services, any organization with complex reporting lines.
_examples: Enterprise ERP — HQ → Regional offices → Departments → Teams, SSO via corporate Okta
Hospital network — Health System → Hospital → Department (ER, Radiology, Pharmacy), SAML with Epic IdP
Government — Agency → Bureau → Division → Branch, strict delegation chains, service accounts for inter-system integration
Financial services — Holding company → Business units → Trading desks, delegation for vacation coverage

service.name: My Enterprise System
service.description: Enterprise system with deep org hierarchy and SSO

tiers.description: Root org — owns permission schema, global admin, platform API keys. No end users here.
Top-level business unit. May have its own SSO provider, admin team, and permission ceiling.
Department within a division. Inherits from division. Can have its own teams, hosted login, and delegation rules.
Arbitrary deeper nesting. parent_id chains as deep as needed. Each sub-unit inherits from its parent via Zanzibar. Visualization supports max_depth up to 10.
tiers._note: This tier is recursive — any org can be parent of any other org via parent_id. The tier array shows the common pattern but the hierarchy is not limited to these levels.

isolation._note: Divisions and departments are isolated by default. A user in Finance cannot see Engineering data. Permission ceilings ensure child orgs cannot exceed parent's permission set.

hosted_login._note: Each division/department can have its own branded login page. No self-registration — all users provisioned via invite or SSO JIT.

_how_it_works.callback: POST /organizations/{org_slug}/auth/sso/callback — validates SAML assertion, JIT creates user, adds to org
_how_it_works.per_org_idp: Each org (division, department) can configure its own SAML IdP. A hospital system might have Epic for clinical, Okta for admin.
_how_it_works.jit: Users authenticated by IdP are auto-created and added to the org. No manual provisioning needed.
_how_it_works.oauth_integration: SSO initiate accepts OAuth params (client_id, redirect_uri, code_challenge) — SSO feeds into the standard OAuth PKCE flow

_how_it_works.api_key_auth: X-API-Key header — org-scoped, permission-scoped, full CRUD at /api-keys/
_how_it_works.password_exempt: Service accounts are exempt from password rotation policy
_how_it_works.use_cases: CI/CD pipelines, inter-service communication, monitoring bots, ETL jobs, automated reports

_how_it_works.act_as: POST /auth/delegate {target_user_id} — returns delegated JWT with both actor and subject
_how_it_works.constraints: Can only delegate permissions YOU have. Cannot delegate to yourself. Default 24h expiry.
_how_it_works.use_cases: Vacation coverage, support access, manager approvals, admin assistance

_how_it_works.nesting: Teams can have parent/child relationships within an org
_how_it_works.inheritance: Team members inherit team permissions via Zanzibar
_how_it_works.use_cases: Cross-department project teams, role-based groups (reviewers, approvers), on-call rotations

_how_it_works.behavior: 200 = authorized (enrichment headers), 401 = no identity, 403 = no permission
_how_it_works.use_case: Edge proxy authorization — protect services without modifying application code

_org_diagram: Root Org (my-enterprise)                                    [level 0 — global admin, API keys]
├── Engineering Division (my-enterprise-eng)               [level 1 — own IdP (Okta), own admin]
│     │     ├── Team: Auth (team, permissions inherited)   [team within dept]
│     │     └── Bob (member, delegated access from Alice during PTO)
└── Bot: Monitoring (service account at root level)
_key_differences_from_departmental: Deep nesting — parent_id chains arbitrarily, not limited to 2 levels
SSO/SAML — per-org IdP with JIT provisioning (departmental has no SSO)
Service accounts — first-class non-human identities with org-scoped API keys
Delegation — scoped, time-limited act-on-behalf with delegated JWTs
Teams — within-org grouping for cross-cutting concerns (departmental uses orgs for everything)
Forward auth — edge proxy integration for zero-code service protection
